findings.evidence: `rg -n "def product|skewSwap" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/AlmostComplex.lean mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Prod.lean` shows `AlmostComplexStructure.product` at `code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/AlmostComplex.lean:138` with `(x,y) ↦ (-y,x)`, and Mathlib `LinearEquiv.skewSwap` at `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Prod.lean:706` with the same map.
findings.fix: Add a bridge/equivalence lemma, or state the real standard-model totally-real lemma for `(AlmostComplexStructure.product V).toLinearMap` in a downstream file that imports both APIs.
findings.issue: The standard model lemmas are stated only for `LinearEquiv.skewSwap`, creating a parallel API to the existing `TauCeti.AlmostComplexStructure.product` standard map on `V × V`.

summary: The core predicate is new, but the standard doubled-module model overlaps TauCeti's existing standard almost-complex structure API and needs a bridge instead of leaving parallel names.
